Ad Text:
We’re seeking an AI Product & Transformation Lead to join a large, customer-focused organisation operating within a complex enterprise environment. This role suits a commercially minded operator who can bridge business and technology, take ownership of AI initiatives end-to-end, and ensure solutions are delivered, adopted, and delivering real impact.

In this role, you’ll be responsible for translating business problems into practical AI solutions, working closely with engineering and data teams to bring them to life, and driving adoption across the organisation. You will play a key role in ensuring AI moves beyond experimentation into embedded, value-generating capability.

Your day-to-day will include:

Identifying and prioritising high-value AI use cases across the business aligned to strategic objectives
Translating business needs into clear solution definitions and working with technical teams to deliver into production
Owning product roadmap, planning, and continuous optimisation of AI solutions
Redesigning workflows to enable effective human and AI collaboration
Driving adoption, change, and training to ensure solutions are used and deliver measurable outcomes
Engaging with stakeholders across multiple business units to align priorities and maximise value
Leading a small team focused on change, enablement, and training
About You:

You are an experienced transformation or product professional who combines strong commercial thinking with delivery capability. You are comfortable operating across business and technology, and have a track record of taking initiatives beyond concept into real-world outcomes.

Ideally, you’ll bring:

Proven experience delivering AI or automation solutions into production, not just strategy or proof of concepts
Background in consulting, digital transformation, or product leadership within complex organisations
Strong experience in stakeholder engagement, operating model change, and driving adoption
Experience owning product roadmaps, prioritisation, and value delivery
Solid understanding of modern AI concepts such as LLMs, automation, and workflow optimisation at a practical level
Ability to work closely with engineering and data teams without needing to be hands-on technical
A pragmatic, delivery-focused mindset with the ability to operate in ambiguity
If you’re seeking a role where you can take ownership of how AI is applied across a business, drive real transformation, and see tangible outcomes from your work, this position offers both impact and visibility.

If you are seeking your next position and would like to discuss this opportunity in further and expanded detail, please reach out directly to Dan Nyhuis at daniel.nyhuis@troocoo.com

About Us:

At Lockheed Martin Australia, we believe that when people are empowered, inspired, and supported, they can achieve extraordinary things. We embrace flexibility, so our people can be their best inside and outside of work. We champion inclusion and continuous learning, ensuring all voices are heard and valued. And we thrive on collaboration, turning challenges into opportunities for growth and innovation. As a leader in national security, our innovative and diverse programs create a more secure future for Australia across space, air, land, sea, and cyber domains. Every day is a chance to solve the world's most complex challenges and make a profound impact. Experience it here.


About the role:

We’re seeking a Lead Software Engineer (Systems Integration & DevSecOps) to play a key role in delivering the AIR6500 integration program, one of Australia’s most advanced Defence capabilities. 

This is a senior, hands-on role where you will own integration outcomes across a complex system-of-systems environment. You’ll lead the design, setup and optimisation of integration environments, bringing together software, infrastructure and mission systems to ensure seamless end-to-end performance. 

Working at the intersection of systems engineering, software integration and DevSecOps, you will shape how integration is executed across the program by driving CI/CD, improving automation, and influencing technical direction while mentoring engineers and uplifting team capability. 

In this role, you will: 

Own and drive integration outcomes across complex, multi-domain systems from planning through to execution

Lead defect analysis and root cause resolution across system, subsystem and component levels

Design, implement and optimise CI/CD pipelines, automation and integration environments

Lead the development and execution of system integration thread tests, including automation strategies

Oversee the set-to-work, configuration, maintenance and monitoring of integration environments (hardware, software, IaC, scripts and test artefacts)

Lead and contribute to test events, showcases and agile planning activities

Mentor and guide engineers while setting technical direction for integration activities

Collaborate across multi-site and international teams to ensure alignment and delivery

  

About you:

You’re an experienced software engineer with deep expertise in systems integration, and you thrive in complex, mission-critical environments. You enjoy solving challenging technical problems, owning outcomes, and influencing how systems are integrated, tested and delivered at scale.

Extensive experience in Systems Integration, Test & Evaluation within complex environments

Strong capability in defect analysis, troubleshooting and system-level problem solving

Deep experience with Linux, networking, scripting and ICT environments

Proven experience implementing CI/CD pipelines, DevSecOps practices and automation

Hands-on experience with virtualisation, containerisation and Infrastructure as Code (e.g. VMware, Kubernetes, Docker, Ansible, Terraform)

Experience establishing and maintaining integration or production environments

Strong understanding of configuration management, safety and quality in I&T environments

Demonstrated ability to lead activities, mentor others and drive outcomes across multidisciplinary teams

An active NV-1 security clearance and ability to handle Export Controlled information 

 

Why Join Us

Work on a next-generation Defence capability at the forefront of system integration

Lead and influence integration strategy and execution across a complex program

Collaborate with high-performing teams across Australia and globally

Access cutting-edge tools, environments and continuous learning opportunities

 


How We Reward and Support You

When you join Lockheed Martin, you work on projects you won’t experience anywhere else.

We offer you a range of benefits built to suit you, no matter what stage of life you are in. 

A variety of flexible work options

Access to our online discounts program offering immediate discounts or cashback

A variety of leave options including Purchase Leave to support you and your family  

Additional benefits such as Annual Flu Vaccinations, Novated Leasing and Fitness Programs


Special Requirements:

Applicants must be eligible to obtain and maintain an Australian Defence Security Clearance and employment may be contingent on the provision of further personal information including citizenship to ensure compliance with international export control legislation and regulations.

A Veteran-friendly Workplace:

Veterans are encouraged to apply. We highly value the unique skills and experiences that veterans and transitioning service members can contribute to our team. We are proud to be recognised as a Veteran Friendly Employer by the Australian Government.

Equal Opportunity Statement:

Lockheed Martin Australia supports Equal Employment Opportunity and values diversity in the workplace by creating a place of employment that is, free of unlawful discrimination and is harassment free, fair, ethical, inclusive, and values the contribution of all its employees. By leveraging our employees' unique talents and experiences, we will deliver innovative, affordable solutions and unparalleled customer value.

As part of our endeavours to increase participation of Indigenous Australians, we welcome applications from all people, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people.

We are committed to creating a workplace where everyone can thrive. If you are selected for this role, we would be happy to discuss any accommodations that will help you to be successful.

Job Title: Senior Professional Services Implementation Engineer, AI
Ad Text:
Position Overview: 
The Professional Services Implementation Engineer, AI is a customer-facing, billable role responsible for delivering specialized AI-driven solutions within the NICE CXone platform. This role blends technical implementation expertise with conversational design leadership, enabling customers to build engaging, effective AI agents, including chatbots, voicebots, knowledge assistants, and multimodal experiences.

Key Responsibilities:
Technical Implementation

Lead complex implementations from pilot to full deployment, ensuring scalable, high-performing AI solutions for enterprise customers.
Configure and build CXone AI solutions, covering NLU, IVR/ACD, ASR, digital channels, and knowledge-driven capabilities.
Develop AI applications (chatbots and voicebots) with a focus on code quality, architectural rigor, and system stability.
Integrate systems seamlessly across CRMs, APIs, and customer ecosystems to create cohesive, end-to-end technical architectures.
Guide successful launches by supporting testing, UAT, go-live readiness, and hypercare with post-deployment optimization.
Solve complex challenges with strong architectural judgment, creative troubleshooting, and a focus on performance.
Collaborate across teams to influence product direction and ensure technical deliverables align with customer requirements.
Enable customers and partners through technical training, configuration workshops, and guidance that builds long-term self-sufficiency.
Create high-impact documentation that clarifies technical concepts and supports the quick resolution of system issues.
Showcase solution value by rapidly assembling functional technical demos that help customers make informed decisions.

Conversational Design & User Experience

Design intuitive AI applications that prioritize user-centric interactions and seamless experiences.
Define user intents, journeys, and decision paths to map out logical and efficient customer interactions.
Create detailed dialog flows, scripts, and interaction models for virtual assistants, ensuring clarity and tone consistency.
Lead conversational design workshops to uncover user needs and align stakeholders on the vision for the experience.
Develop AI assistant personas and multimodal interaction patterns to humanize the interface.
Advise clients on conversational strategies, providing expert guidance on UX best practices and usability standards.

 Project Management & Collaboration:

Partner with project managers on solution design and delivery.
Manage lifecycle development, change control, and risk mitigation.
Deliver enablement for partners and customers.

 Leadership & Internal Enablement:

Mentor colleagues on AI configuration and conversational design.
Produce reusable documentation, templates, and guidelines.
Advocate for AI and design tooling.

 Qualifications:

Education & Experience

Bachelor’s degree in a technical or business field (e.g., Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering) or equivalent work experience.
5+ years of professional experience, including at least 1 year working with digital channels, AI, or bot-driven applications.

Technical & Functional Expertise

Skilled in configuring AI and contact center technologies, including NLU/NLP-powered solutions.
Experience supporting large enterprise environments, including complex integrations and deployments at scale.
Proficient with CRM integrations, APIs, and related ecosystem technologies.
Knowledge of generative AI models, NLU techniques, automation principles, and emerging trends in digital customer engagement.
Familiarity with API testing and documentation tools such as Postman and Swagger.

Solution & Delivery Skills

Ability to design and document workflows using diagramming tools such as Lucidchart or Microsoft Visio.
Experienced in creating detailed use cases, test plans, and UAT guidance.
Skilled in managing customer escalations while maintaining strong client relationships.

Desired Personal Traits

Analytical, inquisitive, and committed to continuous improvement.
Creative problem-solver able to design solutions for complex challenges.
Customer-centric with strong empathy for end users and business stakeholders.
Team-oriented with exceptional interpersonal and collaboration skills.
Strong facilitation and communication abilities, capable of simplifying complex AI concepts.
Comfortable navigating ambiguity and iterating in fast-paced environments.
Systems thinker with an understanding of how technical components and user experience interconnect.
Early adopter of innovative technologies with a passion for experimentation.
Strong sense of accountability, ownership, and professional presence.
Advocate for the effective use of AI tools to improve decision-making and operational efficiency across the organization.

What’s in It for You? Join an ever-growing, market-disrupting global company where teams of top talent work in a fast-paced, collaborative, and creative environment. NICE offers endless internal career opportunities across multiple roles, disciplines, domains, and locations. If you are passionate about innovation and thrive in dynamic settings, this is your chance to make an impact.

Flexible Work Model: Experience the NICE-FLEX hybrid model with two days in the office and three days of remote work per week, fostering collaboration and innovation while maintaining work-life balance.
About NICE: NICE Ltd. (NASDAQ: NICE) is an innovation leader in AI, cloud, and digital solutions, serving over 25,000 global businesses, including 85 of the Fortune 100 corporations. With over 8,500 employees across 30+ countries, NICE software handles more than 120 million customer interactions and monitors over 3 billion financial transactions daily. NICE is committed to diversity and inclusion, ensuring equal opportunities for all candidates.

Requisition ID: 10120
Reporting To: Tech Manager
Role Type: Individual Contributor

About NiCE

NICE Ltd. (NASDAQ: NICE) software products are used by 25,000+ global businesses, including 85 of the Fortune 100 corporations, to deliver extraordinary customer experiences, fight financial crime and ensure public safety. Every day, NiCE software manages more than 120 million customer interactions and monitors 3+ billion financial transactions.

Known as an innovation powerhouse that excels in AI, cloud and digital, NiCE is consistently recognized as the market leader in its domains, with over 8,500 employees across 30+ countries.

NiCE is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, neurotype, physical or mental disability, veteran status, gender identity, sexual orientation or any other category protected by law.

Job Title: SQL Application Support Analyst ( FTC/ Contract)
Category: Help Desk & IT Support (Information & Communication Technology)
Ad Text:
We are HCLTech, one of the fastest-growing large tech companies in the world and home to 219,000+ people across 54 countries, supercharging progress through industry-leading capabilities centered around Digital, Engineering and Cloud.

About the Role

We are seeking an Engineering Applications Support Analyst to provide technical support across a wide range of engineering and CAD applications. This role will support users across Process, Mechanical, Electrical, Civil, Structural, and CAD disciplines, ensuring stable, efficient, and secure application operations.

Key Responsibilities

Provide support for software installations, upgrades, and configurations

Support engineering and CAD applications used across multiple disciplines

Perform application troubleshooting and issue resolution (L2/L3 support)

Assist with license management and application access issues

Work with SQL Server at a basic level for application support and troubleshooting

Write and execute basic scripts using T‑SQL where required

Collaborate with engineering teams to ensure system availability and performance

Technical Skills & Experience

General experience supporting engineering and CAD applications

Basic to intermediate knowledge of SQL Server

Ability to write and understand T‑SQL scripts

Experience supporting applications such as:

Autodesk Revit, AutoCAD, BIM360, Navisworks, Plant3D

Bentley MicroStation, ProjectWise, OpenRoads, Connection Client

Ansys (including Dyna and License Management)

ArcGIS and extensions

IBM DOORS

Windchill, Teamwork Cloud, Cameo, GENESYS

12d Model, Drains, XP Rafts and Storm, AutoTurn, Strand7, Structural Toolkit, iConstruct, MetroMap

Desired Skills

Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills

Ability to support multiple applications in a complex engineering environment

Good stakeholder communication skills

Experience working in infrastructure, engineering, or construction environments is highly regarded


Why Join HCLTech?

Work with one of the fastest-growing global tech companies.

Diverse workforce with 165 nationalities represented.

Opportunity to collaborate with global teams.

Virtual-first work environment promoting flexibility and work-life balance.

Comprehensive employee benefits and career development programs.

Recognized as a Great Place to Work and Top Employer in 17 countries.

How to apply:

To apply for this role, you should have a full work right to work in Australia .Press "Apply now " button to submit your resume.

HCL's enlightened approach to employee development focus on giving people whatever they need to succeed: be it a virtual assistant or talent transformation sabbaticals; expert guidance or fast track growth; inner peace or democratic empowerment.

Working at Atlassian
Atlassians have the flexibility to work where they can collaborate most effectively with their team, whether at home, one of our global offices, or both.

As a distributed first company, all interviews are conducted virtually. We hire in any country where we have a legal entity. Learn how we work at go.atlassian.com/distributed.

Your Future Team

As a Software Engineer intern, you will use your foundation in computer science and awesome skills in writing software with minimal bugs to tackle hard problems. Whilst passionate about your work and delivering amazing quality, you're still able to go fast, which means using your time wisely for your workload.

As part of a unified R&D team, Engineering is prioritising key initiatives which support our customers in moving to cloud while simultaneously continuing to bring the most value to our customers through investments across our core product suite – including Jira, Confluence, Trello, and Bitbucket.

We're looking for people who want to write the future and who believe that we can accomplish so much more together.

Responsibilities

From day one, you will be a valued part of our development team, trusted to make changes to code that directly impact our products, while gaining deep technical knowledge in full lifecycle product development. You will report to Senior Engineers on your team and dream up and code new features that can be shipped straight into our products.

Minimum Qualifications

Able to commit to a 12-week full-time (40hrs/week) program from November 2026 - February 2027.
Current enrolment in a Bachelors or Masters program in Software Engineering, Computer Science or other related technical program and returning to the program after the completion of the internship, graduating by January 2028.
Programming Foundation: Proficiency in Java, Python, C, C++, or other object-oriented programming languages through coursework or personal projects
Technical Fundamentals: Understanding of data structures, algorithms, and their practical applications in problem-solving

Preferred Qualifications

Software Engineering Experience: Demonstrated skills through previous internships, work experience, personal projects, open-source contributions, or technical publications
AI/ML Interest: Exposure to artificial intelligence concepts, machine learning frameworks, or emerging AI technologies (MCP, agentic AI) through coursework, projects, or self-learning
System Design Awareness: Basic understanding of common design patterns and their applications in software architecture

Benefits and Perks
Atlassian offers a wide range of perks and benefits designed to support you, your family and to help you engage with your local community. Our offerings include health and wellbeing resources, paid volunteer days, and so much more. To learn more, visit Atlassian Internships: Students and New Grads | Atlassian .

About Atlassian 
At Atlassian, we're motivated by a common goal: to unleash the potential of every team. Our software products help teams all over the planet and our solutions are designed for all types of work. Team collaboration through our tools makes what may be impossible alone, possible together.

We believe that the unique contributions of all Atlassians create our success. To ensure that our products and culture continue to incorporate everyone's perspectives and experience, we never discriminate based on race, religion, national origin, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, or marital, veteran, or disability status. Industrial Automation Engineer - Food and Dairy specialist Integrator
This is a strong opportunity for an experienced automation engineer to join a well-regarded industrial automation integrator. Based in Griffith, NSW. The office is small but growing, and the role offers a genuine mix of project delivery and ongoing service and maintenance work. You will work across a range of fast-moving industrial sectors including food and beverage, dairy processing, and FMCG manufacturing. The company has a growing project pipeline and a down to earth, collaborative team environment.

About the Role
You will be involved across all aspects of project delivery, taking projects from design through to commissioning while also supporting key client sites with troubleshooting, optimisation, and upgrade work. Clients are varied and spans modern food production facilities, large-scale dairy plants, and FMCG packaging and processing environments. No two weeks will look the same.

Responsibilities

Develop and configure PLC, HMI, and SCADA systems across food, dairy, FMCG, and general manufacturing sites
Deliver automation projects through design, FAT, SAT, and commissioning
Support client sites with fault-finding, optimisation, and upgrade activities
Produce clean, structured, well-documented code
Work closely with clients and internal teams across both project and service delivery
What We're Looking For

Experienced industrial Automation Engineer
Proven experience in PLC, HMI, and SCADA programming and configuration
Capability across at least two major platforms such as Rockwell, Siemens, Schneider, Omron, GE, or Citect
Background in food and beverage, dairy, FMCG, water and wastewater, energy, or general manufacturing
Experience with FAT, SAT, and commissioning
Strong communication skills and confidence working with technical and operational teams
About the Company
This is an established automation integration business has a long-standing client base across the ditch in New Zealand, but has also developed a stable and growing portfolio of work in the Riverina region - typically food and beverage, dairy processing, FMCG. With steady growth and ongoing investment in people, they offer excellent stability, meaningful project work, and a supportive engineering culture. The Griffith office is a close-knit team where you can make a real impact from day one.

What's on Offer

Salary of $120,000 to 130,000 AUD depending on experience
Full-time, permanent role, Monday to Friday, 40 hours per week
Overtime paid at 1.2x base rate after 45 hours
Health insurance
Birthday leave
Pool vehicles with fuel cards for site work
Flexible time off following extended site work
Collaborative team culture with strong professional development
How to Apply
If you would like to join this growing company, then please apply for the role. If you would like some more information, please reach out to me: Luke Atkinson on +64 4 333 0383 or luke@trs.co.nz

Company Description

About SEEK

SEEK’s portfolio of diverse businesses make a positive impact on a truly global scale. Our purpose is to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and help organisations succeed. We create world-class technology solutions to connect more people to relevant employment, education, small business and volunteer opportunities. We have a culture of high-performance in our workplaces and celebrate the diversity of our employees who contribute to the success of our organisation.

Life at SEEK

SEEK’s purpose is at the centre of everything we do. Our SEEK, which defines the way we work, is all about what makes us unique and a little bit different. Passion, Team, Delivery and Future are our principles that drive innovation and creativity. SEEK strives to support employee wellbeing by providing an amazing experience at work which led us to being named AFR BOSS Top 10 Best Places to Work in Technology (2021-2024). We are proud to work in an environment that's inclusive where everyone's unique ideas, experiences and perspectives are valued.

Our award-winning head office in Cremorne (just a 4 min walk from Richmond station) provides an exceptional space to collaborate with colleagues. The building provides sweeping views of the city, a games area, sit and stand desks at every workstation, modern end-of-trip facilities and Thursday night drinks which gives our people an opportunity to connect in a social setting.

Job Description

The team

At SEEK, our AI Platform Services (AIPS) teams build platforms and solutions that solve complex information problems and enable AI innovation across the SEEK Group. Teams are cross-functional, multi-disciplinary squads that work within an APAC matrix organisational structure. The AI Platform Engineering teams have a specific mission to provide shared infrastructure, governance, and reusable assets that empower AI and product teams to experiment safely, ship faster, and continuously deliver intelligent, differentiated experiences at scale.

You will work with roles across the engineering spectrum from graduate to lead level, including Software Engineers, Engineering Managers, Heads of Engineering, Software Architects, Data Scientists, ML Engineers, Platform Engineers, Product Managers, Business Analysts, and Iteration Managers.

About the role 

A Staff Platform Engineer is a technical lead and senior member within our AI Platform Services (AIPS) Engineering teams. You'll be responsible for the technical strategy and direction of SEEK's GenAI and ML Platforms, ensuring these systems are highly available, resilient, and enable AI innovation at scale. Embedded within a team, you'll lead cross-team technical initiatives that influence AI platform strategy across AIPS and SEEK more broadly. Your primary focus is designing and building AI platform solutions that enable Data Scientists, Engineers, and product teams to deliver AI-powered experiences rapidly and safely. You bring in-depth technical understanding of AI platform infrastructure, multi-cloud architectures (AWS, GCP), and the operational requirements of GenAI and ML systems, while driving alignment of your team's technical roadmap to SEEK's overall AI strategy.
 

Responsibilities 

Design and evolve technical architecture for GenAI and ML platform systems, ensuring they are well-architected, highly available, and aligned with SEEK's development practices and technical metrics
Drive technical strategy that enables SEEK's AI innovation at scale, leading architecture decisions for multi-cloud AI infrastructure (AWS, GCP) and aligning platform capabilities with business objectives
Build and operate highly scalable, performant AI infrastructure using AWS and Python for platform automation, tooling, and integrations while exemplifying clean, well-tested, secure code
Lead cross-team technical initiatives that influence AI platform strategy across AIPS, serving as the key point of contact for technical solutions and system interfaces
Partner with Data Scientists and ML Engineers to enable self-service model development, deployment, and operation while strengthening engineering discipline through understanding of AI/ML operational requirements
Support the Engineering Manager in capacity planning, risk management, and scoping of technical work while building strong relationships with Architecture, Security, and other AI platform teams
Grow team member technical capabilities through coaching on AI/ML platform patterns and practices while fostering a culture of safety, inclusivity, and diversity
Qualifications

Skills and experience 

Proven experience as a Staff Engineer, Principal Engineer, or similar technical leadership role building and operating production platform systems at scale
Strong expertise in cloud infrastructure and platform engineering, with deep hands-on experience in AWS services and multi-cloud architectures
Advanced Python programming skills with demonstrated experience building platform automation, tooling, and integrations for highly scalable systems
Experience with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform), container orchestration (Kubernetes), and observability tools for platform operations
Track record of leading technical roadmaps and driving platform health initiatives aligned with key technical metrics and SRE principles
Experience building platforms that enable self-service capabilities for engineering and data teams, with understanding of developer experience and platform adoption
Hands-on MLOps or ML Engineering experience, GenAI/LLM platform knowledge (model inference, vector databases, RAG patterns), GCP/Vertex AI experience, or ML lifecycle tooling expertise are good to have, but not a must.
Additional Information

Permanent Perks
At SEEK we offer:

Annual Performance Bonus Plan
Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role.
Paid and unpaid leave benefits including Personal Flexi Days and Volunteer Days, as well as the opportunity to purchase additional leave
Support for parents with 14 weeks paid primary carers leave and 2 weeks paid leave for partners
The opportunity to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per financial year

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

We know teams with diverse ideas, experiences and perspectives are more creative and are critical to ensuring effective delivery and innovating to enable our future success. As such, we welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds and life experiences, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages. If you have the skills, curiosity and an adaptable mindset but don't meet every responsibility or qualification listed in this advertisement, please still get in touch with us.

Should you require any specific support or adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond, please advise us and we will be happy to assist.

For this role, only those candidates with the eligible right to work will be considered.

SEEK kindly requests no unsolicited resumes or approaches from recruitment agencies and will not be responsible for any associated fees.

About us

Boeing Defence Australia is shaping the future of aerospace and delivering some of the nation's most important programs for the Australian Defence Force.

Joining us is a chance to make your mark, working with a diverse team that is united in pushing the boundaries of imagination and excellence.

We currently employ more than 2,500 people across 14 sites in Australia and three international locations and have a range of opportunities available as we plan for future growth.

The opportunity

We are seeking a capable engineer or analyst to join the Data Analytics team in Digital Engineering. Our team plays a critical role in delivering data driven insights and scalable data solutions to support key Defence objectives. With an initial focus on establishing reliable data flows into the data warehouse, this role will also contribute across the full data analytics lifecycle, from stakeholder engagement through to delivery of impactful visualisations and AI ready data products.  The role offers technical mentoring within a supportive team, and opportunities to progress into advanced analytics, AI enablement and broader platform engineering activities. 

Responsibilities:

Collaborate with cross functional teams to understand data needs and deliver insights and fit for purpose data solutions.
Undertake data wrangling, extraction, transformation and loading utilising Python and SQL.
Develop and maintain reliable data pipelines and ETL/ELT processes in Apache Airflow that connect large datasets from a variety of structured and unstructured sources.
Utilise dbt for data modelling and transformation.
Support development and maintenance of data platform infrastructure, including data warehouses, data lakes and, where required, data access APIs.
Build visualisations in Tableau.
Develop data applications in Python/HTML/CSS.
Work with stakeholders to define requirements, support delivery, and promote data standards, reuse, scalability, stability and operational efficiency.
Contribute to automation and CI/CD practices to improve scalability, repeatability and reliability of data and analytics solutions.

Experience/Qualifications

Essential:

Bachelor's degree in a technical field (e.g., Software Engineering, Computer Science, Physics, Mathematics) or equivalent industry experience.
Demonstrated experience in Python.
Ability to work with SQL (knowledge will be valued; training will be provided).
Understanding of data modelling principles.
Experience or strong interest in building and maintaining data pipelines and transforming data from multiple sources.
Ability to work collaboratively with stakeholders to understand requirements and deliver high quality data products.
Strong problem solving skills and attention to data quality, reliability and documentation.

Desirable:

Defence and/or Aerospace domain knowledge.
Experience with dbt modelling and Apache Airflow.
Familiarity with MLOps and CI/CD practices.
Experience with Tableau.
Experience with Docker.
Experience with cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP).
Experience with data warehousing, data lakes, or data platform infrastructure.
Familiarity with data quality monitoring, observability and pipeline testing practices.
Understanding of security, governance and access control considerations in enterprise data environments.
Analytical aptitude demonstrated through previous projects.

Culture

We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace. Female applicants, people of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Island descent and ex-defence personnel are encouraged to apply.

Benefits

Flexible working options
Study assistance
Salary packaging
Employee Incentive Program              
Global opportunities

Ad Text:

Experience developing and deploying models using deep learning frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch, or JAX).

Master's degree in computer science, mathematics, or a related technical field.
Experience as an IT infrastructure consultant or enterprise architect working in data center investment strategies and proposals.
Experience with AI Infrastructure systems, networking technologies (e.g., DPU, RoCE, InfiniBand), cooling, and accelerators, GPUs and TPUs.
Experience in leveraging main AI and software stacks and platforms to bring up and deploy AI compute clusters.
Knowledge of the AI infrastructure market, including main technology providers, differentiators and trends.
Ability to work and grow in fluid environments.
About the job


When leading companies choose Google Cloud, it's a huge win for spreading the power of cloud computing globally. Once educational institutions, government agencies, and other businesses sign on to use Google Cloud products, you come in to facilitate making their work more productive, mobile, and collaborative. You listen and deliver what is most helpful for the customer. You assist fellow sales Googlers by problem-solving key technical issues for our customers. You liaise with the product marketing management and engineering teams to stay on top of industry trends and devise enhancements to Google Cloud products.

In this role, you will work to understand the needs of customers and help shape the future using AI technology. You will work with Google Cloud Platform's technology, complete AI stack, and will position the same to customers in all verticals. You will support Google Cloud sales teams to pilot, and deploy Google Cloud’s AI/ML accelerators (TPU/GPU) at AI innovators, large enterprises, and early stage AI startups. You will help customers innovate with solutions using Google Cloud’s flexible and open AI infrastructure.

You will be working with Google customers on AI Infrastructure servers and networking infrastructure deployments. You will guide customer discussions on network topologies, compute/storage and support bring up of server/network/cluster/cooling deployments. You will serve as a technical expert on the Google Cloud AI infrastructure, guiding customers through the architecture, deployment, and optimization of cost-efficient training and inference jobs on Cloud TPUs.

Google Cloud accelerates every organization’s ability to digitally transform its business and industry. We deliver enterprise-grade solutions that leverage Google’s cutting-edge technology, and tools that help developers build more sustainably. Customers in more than 200 countries and territories turn to Google Cloud as their trusted partner to enable growth and solve their most critical business problems.

Responsibilities

Become a trusted advisor to customers, helping them understand and incorporate AI accelerators into their overall cloud and IT strategy by designing training and inferencing platforms.
Demonstrate how Google Cloud is differentiated, highlighting the power of accelerators by working with customers on POCs, demonstrating features, optimizing model performance, profiling, and bench marking.
Design and implement multi-host AI training and inferencing solutions on Google Cloud TPUs, focusing on scalability and performance tuning.
Conduct performance profiling and optimization of customer models and data pipelines for the TPU architecture, identifying and resolving issues.
Advise customers on best practices for integrating their MLOps workflows with the Google Cloud AI Platform ecosystem for TPU utilization.
Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. Job Description


Join the team redefining how the world experiences design.

Hey, g'day, mabuhay, kia ora, 你好, hallo, vítejte!
Thanks for stopping by. We know job hunting can be a little time consuming and you're probably keen to find out what's on offer, so we'll get straight to the point.

Where and how you can work

Our flagship campus is in Sydney. We also have a campus in Melbourne and co-working spaces across Australia. But you have choice in where and how you work. We trust our Canvanauts to choose the balance that empowers them and their team to achieve their goals.

What you'd be doing in this role

As Canva scales change continues to be part of our DNA. But we like to think that's all part of the fun. So this will give you the flavour of the type of things you'll be working on when you start, but this will likely evolve.

At the moment, this role is focused on:

Partnering with product, engineering, and machine learning teams to embed localisation quality signals earlier in the development cycle
Establishing and maintaining documentation on localisation AI tooling, prompt libraries, and evaluation methodologies
Leading the refinement of our AI translation pipeline, driving optimisation through prompt engineering and rigorous evaluation processes while determining the optimal balance between AI automation and human translation workflows.
Contributing to the overall localisation strategy by surfacing data-driven insights from evaluations and automation systems
Establishing processes with country teams to propagate learnings across all regions and optimise operations
Creating and iterating on team strategy, clearly communicating how that strategy rolls up into larger organisational goals and responding quickly to shifts in priorities and timelines
Building trust and collaborative culture with country teams and product teams within Canva and shape prioritisation

You're probably a match if

You have deep expertise in localisation frameworks and technologies; bonus marks for Smartling experience and LLM evals
You have a builder's mindset, meaning you're comfortable vibe coding scripts and generally just having a go at new things
You love taking things from chaos to clarity and you have the drive to see things through despite changes and setbacks
You understand how user experience impacts growth and you can connect the user journey to opportunities turning localisation initiatives into measurable signals
You understand the needs of LOTE customers and the opportunities available to meet those needs, ideally from experience working in a localisation/internationalisation environment within a SaaS company
You can document complex systems simply and clearly for broader audiences
Speaking multiple languages is a huge bonus

About the team

The Localisation group is responsible for the localisation of Canva’s product, marketing, and content as well as the tools, processes, and assets that make this mission possible. We sit within the Global Content and Discovery supergroup but we work closely with stakeholders across the whole organisation — from customer support and sales through to our AI features and the core editing product.

Localisation is made up of localisation specialists, designers, and language managers (native speaker language experts) who work across the business to achieve our mission of equipping all Canvanauts with the best practices, insights and processes they need to go global.

Every month, more than 100 million Canva users create a design in one of the 100 languages other than English that Canva supports. The localisation specialty is made up of three  teams of linguists, project managers, and designers distributed throughout the company with a mission of celebrating the diversity of our growing base of global users and making their Canva experiences delightful. 

This is a senior specialist role within the Localisation Group where you will be partnering with country teams, product teams, and AI/ML stakeholders to build the technical infrastructure for LOTE at Canva as well as robust processes to make sure Canva is LOTE-ready from the get go. You'll drive the design and execution of evaluation frameworks, promote innovation across the team and automate systems that improve the quality, speed, and scalability of localisation across all markets. This is a hands-on, high-autonomy role for someone who loves building things that didn't exist before and has a knack for spinning multiple plates at once. You thrive in an environment where priorities shift, initiatives stack up, and no two weeks look the same.

What's in it for you?

Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard - and we do - but you'll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a stack of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work.

Here's a taste of what's on offer:

Equity packages - we want our success to be yours too
Inclusive parental leave policy that supports all parents & carers
An annual Vibe & Thrive allowance to support your wellbeing, social connection, office setup & more
Flexible leave options that empower you to be a force for good, take time to recharge and supports you personally

Check out lifeatcanva.com for more info.

Other stuff to know

We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Canva and our culture. When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process.

Please note that interviews are conducted virtually.

Our flagship campus is in Sydney. We also have a campus in Melbourne and co-working spaces in Brisbane, Perth and Adelaide. But you have choice in where and how you work, we trust our Canvanauts to choose the balance that empowers them and their team to achieve their goals.

Job Description


What you’d be doing in this role

As Canva scales change continues to be part of our DNA. But we like to think that's all part of the fun. So this will give you the flavour of the type of things you'll be working on when you start, but this will likely evolve.

At the moment, this role is focused on:

• Leading a new team pioneering AI developer tooling experience to boost engineering productivity at scale
• Driving the strategy and execution of Canva’s “AI-Orchestrated Developer Experience” goal
• Building agentic, context-aware AI systems that understand and integrate into our software development lifecycle
• Partnering with platform and product teams to embed intelligent capabilities across the entire developer workflow
• Mentoring and growing a high-performing team of engineers working on the frontier of AI and developer experience
• Evaluating and trialling emerging AI tools and shaping their impact across Canva Engineering

You're probably a match if

• You’ve led high-impact engineering teams and are passionate about developer tooling/experience
• You have deep experience with AI developer tooling and a good understanding of AI and AI models
• You thrive in ambiguity and love building 0→1 systems that scale
• You’re a collaborative leader who can influence across teams and drive outcomes without authority
• You care about building a magical developer experience and are excited to push boundaries

About the team

The AI Developer Tooling Experience team is part of the DevX group within Canva’s Infra specialty. Our mission? Empower every Canva engineer to do the best work of their lives — faster, smarter, and with less toil. We’re evolving our tools beyond individual AI helpers into orchestrated workflows and intelligent multi-agent systems that know our codebase, conventions, and context. This is a greenfield space — and your chance to lead the future of AI-powered software development at Canva.

What's in it for you?

Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard - and we do - but you'll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a range of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work.

Here's a taste of what's on offer:

Equity packages - we want our success to be yours too

Inclusive parental leave policy that supports all parents & carers

An annual Vibe & Thrive allowance to support your wellbeing, social connection, office setup & more

Flexible leave options that empower you to be a force for good, take time to recharge and supports you personally

Check out lifeatcanva.com for more info.

Other stuff to know

We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Canva and our culture. When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process.

We celebrate all types of skills and backgrounds at Canva so even if you don’t feel like your skills quite match what’s listed above - we still want to hear from you!

Please note that interviews are conducted virtually.

Company Description

Job Description

The team

At SEEK, our AI Platform Services (AIPS) teams build platforms and solutions that solve complex information problems and enable AI innovation across the SEEK Group. Teams are cross-functional, multi-disciplinary squads that work within an APAC matrix organisational structure. The AI Platform Engineering teams have a specific mission to provide shared infrastructure, governance, and reusable assets that empower AI and product teams to experiment safely, ship faster, and continuously deliver intelligent, differentiated experiences at scale.

You will work with roles across the engineering spectrum from graduate to lead level, including Software Engineers, Engineering Managers, Heads of Engineering, Software Architects, Data Scientists, ML Engineers, Platform Engineers, Product Managers, Business Analysts, and Iteration Managers.

About the role 

A Staff Platform Engineer is a technical lead and senior member within our AI Platform Services (AIPS) Engineering teams. You'll be responsible for the technical strategy and direction of SEEK's GenAI and ML Platforms, ensuring these systems are highly available, resilient, and enable AI innovation at scale. Embedded within a team, you'll lead cross-team technical initiatives that influence AI platform strategy across AIPS and SEEK more broadly. Your primary focus is designing and building AI platform solutions that enable Data Scientists, Engineers, and product teams to deliver AI-powered experiences rapidly and safely. You bring in-depth technical understanding of AI platform infrastructure, multi-cloud architectures (AWS, GCP), and the operational requirements of GenAI and ML systems, while driving alignment of your team's technical roadmap to SEEK's overall AI strategy.

Responsibilities 

Design and evolve technical architecture for GenAI and ML platform systems, ensuring they are well-architected, highly available, and aligned with SEEK's development practices and technical metrics
Drive technical strategy that enables SEEK's AI innovation at scale, leading architecture decisions for multi-cloud AI infrastructure (AWS, GCP) and aligning platform capabilities with business objectives
Build and operate highly scalable, performant AI infrastructure using AWS and Python for platform automation, tooling, and integrations while exemplifying clean, well-tested, secure code
Lead cross-team technical initiatives that influence AI platform strategy across AIPS, serving as the key point of contact for technical solutions and system interfaces
Partner with Data Scientists and ML Engineers to enable self-service model development, deployment, and operation while strengthening engineering discipline through understanding of AI/ML operational requirements
Support the Engineering Manager in capacity planning, risk management, and scoping of technical work while building strong relationships with Architecture, Security, and other AI platform teams
Grow team member technical capabilities through coaching on AI/ML platform patterns and practices while fostering a culture of safety, inclusivity, and diversity
Qualifications

Skills and experience 

Proven experience as a Staff Engineer, Principal Engineer, or similar technical leadership role building and operating production platform systems at scale
Strong expertise in cloud infrastructure and platform engineering, with deep hands-on experience in AWS services and multi-cloud architectures
Advanced Python programming skills with demonstrated experience building platform automation, tooling, and integrations for highly scalable systems
Experience with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform), container orchestration (Kubernetes), and observability tools for platform operations
Track record of leading technical roadmaps and driving platform health initiatives aligned with key technical metrics and SRE principles
Experience building platforms that enable self-service capabilities for engineering and data teams, with understanding of developer experience and platform adoption
Hands-on MLOps or ML Engineering experience, GenAI/LLM platform knowledge (model inference, vector databases, RAG patterns), GCP/Vertex AI experience, or ML lifecycle tooling expertise are good to have, but not a must.
Additional Information

Permanent Perks
At SEEK we offer:

Annual Performance Bonus Plan
Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role.
Paid and unpaid leave benefits including Personal Flexi Days and Volunteer Days, as well as the opportunity to purchase additional leave
Support for parents with 14 weeks paid primary carers leave and 2 weeks paid leave for partners
Tailored career development planning (including Education Assistance Program)

About Us

Future Secure AI is a rapidly scaling AI company co‑developed with one of the world’s largest financial institutions. Operating with the agility of a startup and the backing of global enterprise leaders, we are building technology that elevates human performance through advanced AI Co-workers and next‑generation LLM systems.

With deep C‑suite access across industries and a growing network of global partners, our mission is bold: to redefine how humans and AI collaborate—and deliver 10x outcomes for individuals and organisations.

We are growing fast and looking for exceptional talent to help shape the future of AI.

The Opportunity

As a Senior Data Scientist, you will be a key technical leader within our advanced analytics and AI group. You will design, build, and deploy sophisticated machine learning systems—particularly LLM‑driven solutions—that solve high‑value, real‑world problems for enterprise clients.

You’ll be part of a culture that prizes technical excellence, creativity, and continuous learning. Beyond technical depth, you will influence strategy, mentor emerging talent, and help shape our approach to responsible and scalable AI.

Your work will directly contribute to AI systems that have the potential to transform industries—and society.

What You’ll Do

Architect, build, and deploy machine learning models and LLM systems that drive meaningful impact for our products and users

Analyse complex, large‑scale structured and unstructured datasets to uncover insights and opportunities

Enhance LLM output quality across diverse use cases, including optimisation of multi‑step reasoning chains

Lead MLOps initiatives including model performance tracking, benchmark design, quality assurance, and compliance

Influence long‑term data and AI strategy, including governance, ethical AI, and scalable frameworks

Collaborate closely with engineering, product, and executive stakeholders to deliver innovative, production‑ready solutions

Mentor and uplift junior data scientists, setting standards for excellence

About You

You are a seasoned Data Scientist (5+ years) with deep hands‑on experience and a track record of delivering impactful ML and AI outcomes. You thrive in ambiguity, communicate exceptionally well, and enjoy partnering with cross‑functional teams to deliver measurable results.

You balance scientific rigour with practical execution, and you bring a curiosity that keeps you constantly exploring new research—especially in the fast‑moving LLM space.

You’ll be successful if you have:

A Master’s or Doctorate in Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science or a related discipline

Experience from within AI sector, ideally agentic AI

Strong experience with NLP techniques: LSTMs, CNNs, transformers, topic modelling, supervised NLP

Commercial experience with modern LLM ecosystems (e.g., LangChain, LlamaIndex, RAG pipelines, multi‑agent frameworks, GPT‑based systems)

Python experience focused on machine learning and NLP. Knowledge of other software languages is highly beneficial

Ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear, actionable insights for non‑technical stakeholders

Empathy, humility, and a growth mindset—open to feedback and committed to collaboration

Comfort working in an agile, fast‑evolving environment

Ability to attend our Sydney CBD office 1–2 days per week

Why Join Us?

Shape the future of AI alongside world‑class technical and commercial leaders

Work with cutting‑edge models, frameworks, and R&D pipelines

Contribute to solutions used by global enterprises

Grow rapidly in a company with significant backing and momentum

Join a high‑trust, high‑performance culture built around craft, ownership, and impact

If you’re driven by innovation, inspired by big challenges, and ready to build AI systems that matter—we’d love to meet you.

For a quicker response, we encourage you to apply directly through our website at https://www.futuresecure.ai/. There, you can watch a video about AI workers and explore the career opportunities we have available.

Company Description


Nearmap is a global property intelligence company — the only complete property intelligence solution and a single, trusted source of truth for property decisions.

Job Description


To help us scale the AI systems behind that intelligence, we're looking for a Senior MLOps Engineer to join our AICV Platform team in Sydney.

This is a genuine opportunity to own greenfield platform work at serious scale. Nearmap is one of the top three AWS users in the APAC region. The systems you'll build aren't theoretical — they're processing aerial imagery across Australia, the US, and Canada, and serving AI models to real customers at high concurrency.

You'll sit between traditional DevOps and the new world of AI engineering — which means this role suits someone with a strong back-end and platform engineering foundation who is ready to grow their career into AI, or who is already making that transition. AI experience is a bonus, not a barrier to entry — we can coach that side as a team.

🔹 What you'll be doing

You'll own projects end to end — from architecture and design through to implementation, delivery, and production operations. That kind of trust and autonomy is real here, not a recruitment line.

Day to day that looks like:

Designing and building scalable ML infrastructure — large-scale batch inference on EKS, real-time model serving via Ray Serve, and GPU-accelerated distributed training on GCP and AWS
Building the LLM platform that will underpin Nearmap's next generation of generative AI products — including deployment, governance, experimentation, and cost optimisation across CPU/GPU workloads
Owning developer experience for AICV engineering teams — CI/CD pipelines, Docker tooling, ArgoCD, W&B, and Arize AI
Driving reliability and observability across production ML systems with real SLAs and SLOs
Contributing production-quality Python to shared platform libraries and services
Playing a tech lead role on your primary project, with advisory exposure across others
Engaging directly with ML tooling vendors including AWS, GCP, Weights & Biases, Confluent, and Arize AI

The teams depending on your platform include Model R&D, Computer Vision, Insurance Data Science, Agentic AI, and AI Map Data.

Qualifications


What we're looking for

Must-haves:

Production experience building and operating Kubernetes-based platforms (EKS/GKE) at scale
High-scale, high-concurrency API development and operation in production environments
Real operational mindset: you've managed systems with SLAs and SLOs and stayed close through incidents
Strong DevOps fundamentals — CI/CD, infrastructure-as-code, deployment pipelines
Production-level Python — services and libraries, not just scripts

Highly valued:

Ray Serve or equivalent real-time model serving experience
LLM or generative AI platform work in production (beyond PoC)
ML observability tooling — Arize AI, Evidently, Prometheus/Grafana
Infrastructure-as-code with Terraform
Experience at terabyte or petabyte scale
GPU/CPU cost optimisation

A degree in computer science, engineering, mathematics, or a related field is welcome — equivalent practical experience is equally valued.

This role is based in our Barangaroo, Sydney office, with an expectation of at least two days per week on-site.

This role is open to Australian citizens and permanent residents, or those with full working rights in Australia.

Senior Software Engineer, AI Development

Location: Australia (remote, AU-based)

Employment type: Full-time

Salary: Competitive senior market rate




The problem space

Refactor are partnering with an Australian AI startup with a production AI platform, enterprise customers and a growing SaaS offering.




We’re now extending the platform to support an LLM-agnostic, agentic AI assistant that integrates across enterprise systems and helps everyday business users plan work, prioritise tasks, and structure their work experience.




This role exists because the system is no longer experimental. Retrieval quality, orchestration, latency, security and UX are all priorities in our ongoing product development.




What we’re building

The platform is built around production-grade RAG and agentic workflows.




Backend:




A Java-based API platform that:




Orchestrates LLM calls
Executes multi-step agent workflows
Integrates with enterprise systems
Enforces tenancy, security and access controls




Retrieval layer

Weaviate as the primary vector database
Hybrid search (vector + keyword)
Tuned chunking, embeddings and reranking




Frontend:

React application used daily by non-technical business users
Surfaces agent outputs, plans, tasks and recommendations




AI layer

LLM-agnostic (supporting models from Anthropic, Mistral & Open AI)
Support for cloud, private and sovereign deployments
Explicit cost, latency and reliability controls




The assistant is agentic by design. The planning, sequencing actions, querying multiple systems, and producing structured outputs users can act on.




What you’ll be responsible for:




You’ll be a hands-on senior engineer contributing across backend and frontend, with a primary focus on core product development.




Backend & product engineering:

Strong T-shaped engineering skills with deep expertise in backend Java development and practical knowledge across related technologies required to deliver production-ready systems
Experience with AWS and cloud technologies, CI/CD pipelines, system configuration, unit and manual testing, and integrations with external systems such as web crawlers and enterprise API services
Developing LLM orchestration and agent execution layers
Implementing and maintaining enterprise integrations (documents, tasks, calendars, knowledge systems)
Ensuring APIs are versioned, stable and suitable for enterprise time horizons




RAG & retrieval systems:

Designing and evolving production RAG pipelines




Owning:

Document ingestion and preprocessing
Chunking strategies and embedding lifecycle management
Retrieval tuning, hybrid search and reranking
Experience in database-level optimisations, query and retrieval performance tuning, and embedding lifecycle management to improve accuracy, consistency, and efficiency in production datasets




Improving grounding, relevance and consistency across real customer datasets
Handling retrieval failure modes and partial or stale data scenarios




System quality, scale & reliability

Optimising latency, throughput and cost across retrieval and model calls
Implementing observability (structured logging, metrics, tracing)
Building guardrails, timeouts and fallback behaviour for agent workflows
Expertise in observability, structured logging, metrics, tracing, and automated testing strategies to ensure robust, scalable, and reliable production systems
Contributing to tenant isolation, data boundaries and security controls




Frontend contribution (important, but secondary):




Practical understanding of frontend requirements, enabling close collaboration with UI teams to align API design and deliver fully integrated, production-ready features
Translating backend capabilities into clear, usable interfaces for non-technical users
Contributing directly to the React codebase where appropriate
Ensuring tight alignment between API design and frontend needs




React experience is not required, but practical exposure is a strong advantage.




Product-driven engineering

Translating real user workflows into concrete product features
Making pragmatic trade-offs between accuracy, explainability, performance and UX
Iterating based on production feedback, not assumptions




What we’re looking for




We’re looking for engineers who have already built and managed sovereign AI deployments in production environments.




Required:

5+ years professional software engineering experience
2+ years building RAG-based systems used in production
Strong backend experience




Hands-on experience with:
Vector databases
Embeddings, chunking and retrieval strategies
LLM orchestration (framework-based or custom)
Experience supporting production, customer-facing systems




Valued:

Experience with retrieval quality and failure modes
Experience with agentic workflows beyond simple chains
SaaS or multi-tenant architectures
React experience or strong API-first frontend collaboration
Enterprise or regulated environments
AWS, private cloud or hybrid deployments

You’ll be involved with evolving the backend architecture and user-facing product

Australian company building globally relevant enterprise AI
